caveats: Informational and educational only; not medical advice.
Does not diagnose disease or determine whether a person is infected.
Not a treatment recommendation or substitute for a healthcare professional.
Relative Maryland county seasonal Lyme forecast, not a per-bite infection probability.
Displayed county-week values are derived seasonal allocations of annual county forecasts, not observed county-week data.
Static seasonal prior; not a weather-adjusted forecast.
CDC national onset seasonality is not county-specific.
Surveillance/reporting changes and interventions are unmodeled.
Empirical intervals describe forecast uncertainty, not clinical confidence for an individual bite.

seasonal_allocation.role: allocates the annual county forecast across MMWR weeks; it is not observed county-week truth

signals_used: CDC national Lyme onset seasonality for display allocation

target.interpretation: reported surveillance incidence used as a proxy for relative Lyme disease pressure

uncertainty.interpretation: Intervals describe historical forecast residual uncertainty around reported-incidence proxy forecasts, not clinical certainty for a person or bite.

new_observed_data_steps: ingest reviewed county-year surveillance data with source caveats
compare the prior forecast with the newly observed county-year outcome
record residuals by county, branch, surveillance regime, and region
refit eligible forecast branches when coverage is complete enough
regenerate forecasts, intervals, scores, and source notes
update_policy.promotion_rule: Bayesian or calibration updates are not promoted to public scores unless rolling-origin backtests show improved error or calibration.

guidance_links.title: CDC: What to do after a tick bite
CDC: Lyme disease signs and symptoms

temporal_contract.historical_display_policy: Historical years are observed annual county incidence only; derived weekly or monthly allocations must not be labeled as observed historical risk.
